title: Challenge Lab - Build API Gateway with Lambda Integration

tasks.title: Sign into the AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to access the AWS Console in a new tab.
On the AWS sign-in page, leave the Account ID as default, copy your Username and Password from the Lab Console, and sign in.
Set the AWS Region to US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Sign into the AWS Management Console

XYZ Company wants to build a serverless API using Amazon API Gateway with AWS Lambda integration to process requests. Your job is to create a Lambda function, set up an API with a resource and method, integrate it with Lambda, deploy the API, and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Choose Author from scratch.
Select runtime as Python 3.12.
Select create new role from aws policy templates
Choose Basic Lambda @ Edge Permission (For CloudFront Trigger)
Select REST API
Create a Get Method
Configure the method: Integration type: Lambda Function. Enable Use Lambda Proxy integration. Lambda Region: us-east-1. Lambda Function: Select LambdaFunction.
Integration type: Lambda Function.
Enable Use Lambda Proxy integration.
Lambda Region: us-east-1.
Lambda Function: Select LambdaFunction.
Grant API Gateway permission to invoke the Lambda function if prompted.
Test the method:
Create a new stage (e.g., prod).
Note the Invoke URL for the deployed API (e.g., https://<api-id>.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/prod/greet).
Access the URL in a browser or a tool like curl to verify the Lambda response
Confirm the Lambda function and API are created.
Verify the API resource /greet with the GET method is integrated with the Lambda function.
Ensure the API is deployed to the prod stage and the invoke URL returns the expected Lambda response.
Click Validate in the lab console to check the setup.
Sign out of AWS by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
Click End Lab in the lab console to complete the lab.
